requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must have qualifying children under age 17 and income below phase-out thresholds to claim Child Tax Credit providing up to $2,000 per child credit with refundable Additional Child Tax Credit portion for lower-income families
criteria: Qualifying child must be under age 17 at end of tax year
Child must be dependent: son, daughter, stepchild, foster child, sibling, or descendant
Child must have lived with you more than half the year and not provided over half own support
Child must be U.S. citizen, national, or resident alien with valid Social Security number
2024 credit: $2,000 per qualifying child under age 17
Credit phases out: $200,000 AGI (single/head of household), $400,000 (married filing jointly)
Up to $1,700 (2024) refundable as Additional Child Tax Credit if earned income exceeds $2,500
Credit for other dependents (age 17+): $500 non-refundable credit for qualifying dependents

child_tax_credit_rules.credit_amount: $2,000 per qualifying child under 17; $500 for other qualifying dependents
child_tax_credit_rules.refundable_portion: Up to $1,700 refundable (Additional Child Tax Credit) if earned income over $2,500
child_tax_credit_rules.income_thresholds: Full credit if income under $200,000 (single/HOH) or $400,000 (MFJ)
child_tax_credit_rules.phase_out_rate: $50 reduction for each $1,000 income over threshold
child_tax_credit_rules.ssn_requirement: Qualifying child must have SSN issued before due date of return to qualify
child_tax_credit_rules.dollar_for_dollar: Credit reduces tax liability dollar-for-dollar; more valuable than deduction

restrictions: Age limit strictly enforced: child must be under 17 on December 31 of tax year
ITIN not sufficient for child; must have valid Social Security number for $2,000 credit
Phase-out reduces credit by $50 for each $1,000 over income threshold; can eliminate credit entirely
Modified AGI includes excluded foreign earned income for phase-out calculation
Cannot claim credit if married filing separately in most cases
Child must meet relationship, age, residency, support, and dependent tests
Divorced/separated parents: only custodial parent can claim unless Form 8332 releases exemption
Credit claimed on Form 1040, Schedule 8812; Additional Child Tax Credit on Form 8812 if refundable portion
